relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 39-year-old African American woman with chronic focal epilepsy presented with three days of fevers (>38 degreesC) and a mildly pruritic, erythematous maculopapular rash. The
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Severe drug-induced hypersensitivity syndrome causing systemic collapse with multiorgan involvement. Can cause refractory hypotension, shock, and hemoglobin drop through hemolysis/bone marrow suppression - mimics post-procedural drug reactions.

relevant_cases.diagnosis: Idiopathic non-clonal mast cell activation syndrome

relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Systemic mast cell activation can cause acute hypotension, syncope, and hemodynamic collapse similar to tamponade-like physiology. Demonstrates systemic cause of refractory shock without structural cardiac abnormality.
